title: Suspicious person prompts lockdown, canceled classes at R.I. college
author: Carson Lyle
bullets: Community College of Rhode Island's Warwick campus locked down due to a report of a suspicious man with a possible weapon.
Warwick police searched with K-9 units and drones, found a backpack containing an airsoft gun and pellet rifle.
Classes and activities were canceled, lockdown lifted later; police have identified the person of interest, still searching.

title: Red Sox get more bad news after Triston Casas suffers setback in his recovery
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Triston Casas' recovery from a knee injury has been stalled by a rib muscle strain.
Red Sox manager Alex Cora reported Casas' attempt to resume hitting did not go well.
Casas will undergo imaging this week to assess the rib muscle injury.

title: What to know about the ‘massive’ military bunker beneath Trump’s ballroom
author: New York Times Service
bullets: Trump plans a $400 million ballroom above a new underground military complex at the White House.
The Presidential Emergency Operations Center is being replaced to include bomb shelters and medical facilities.
Secret Service documents stress the importance of completing the project to ensure White House security.

title: Just one Mass. college ranks among the nation’s best graduate schools, per U.S. News
author: Autumn Sloboda
bullets: MIT ranked No. 1 for best graduate engineering program in U.S. News 2026 rankings.
Massachusetts medical and nursing programs did not rank in top tiers for research or primary care.
Boston University's Sargent College maintained its top ranking in occupational therapy for the fifth year.

title: Suffolk DA Kevin Hayden appears to have a new primary challenger. She used to work in his office.
author: Ross Cristantiello
bullets: Linda Champion, former aide to Suffolk DA Kevin Hayden, filed paperwork indicating a potential challenge in the upcoming election.
Champion ran for Suffolk DA in 2018, finishing fifth in the Democratic primary, and served under Hayden in 2023.
Hayden faces criticism from law enforcement over manslaughter charges against a Boston police officer, seen by some as politically motivated.

title: Malden cook goes viral for selling trendy Ilocos empanadas out of her apartment
author: Katelyn Umholtz
bullets: Janz Van's Ilocos empanadas went viral, attracting customers from the US and Canada via TikTok exposure.
Malden's Board of Health stopped Van's operations, citing a zoning ordinance against residential kitchen permits.
Van plans to sell her empanadas at pop-up locations and is fundraising for a $30,000 food truck.

title: Giannis Antetokounmpo paid high praise to Celtics’ coach Joe Mazzulla
author: Hayden Bird
bullets: Giannis Antetokounmpo praised Celtics coach Joe Mazzulla for his mentality and leadership during challenging circumstances.
Mazzulla led the Celtics to second place in the Eastern Conference despite injuries and roster changes.
Antetokounmpo expressed a desire to stay in Milwaukee, emphasizing the need for a winning culture.

title: Person seriously injured in fall at Firefighting Academy facility
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: A person was seriously injured in a fall at the Firefighting Academy in Stow, Massachusetts, on Tuesday morning.
First responders and additional ambulances provided medical care at the scene; a helicopter was unable to respond due to weather.
The injured patient was transported by ground to UMass Memorial Medical Center in Worcester; their identity remains undisclosed.

title: TravelMag went on a seafood road trip in Maine and named these ‘can’t-miss’ restaurants. Which is your favorite?
author: Kristi Palma
bullets: TravelMag highlighted 10 'can't-miss' seafood restaurants in Maine after a road trip along the coast.
Featured stops include The Clam Shack in Kennebunkport, Eventide Oyster Co. in Portland, and Bagaduce Lunch in Penobscot.
TravelMag praised The Clam Shack’s fried clams and Bagaduce Lunch as a hidden gem in the seafood world.

title: Ex-Stoughton cop Matthew Farwell’s DNA was on strap used to strangle Sandra Birchmore, prosecutors say
author: Ross Cristantiello
bullets: Matthew Farwell's DNA was found on a strap used to strangle Sandra Birchmore, according to prosecutors.
Farwell is accused of killing Birchmore in 2021, alleging the death as suicide; he believed she was pregnant with his child.
Despite initial exclusion from fatherhood claims, Farwell thought he fathered Birchmore's child; his DNA matched crime scene evidence.
